meta.name: Telegram Trade Alerts
meta.description: Sends Telegram notifications when forward test trades open or close. Fire-and-forget (never blocks cron), rich HTML formatting, zero new dependencies.

motivation.problem: Forward test runs hourly via cron. The only way to know if a trade happened is to open the /live dashboard and check. With ~1 trade per 5 days per coin, most dashboard checks show no change. This creates unnecessary friction and anxiety.
motivation.value: Push notification on trade events means zero-effort monitoring. Phone buzzes when something happens, silence when nothing does. Perfect for a multi-week forward test.
motivation.audience: Strategy owner who needs to know about trade events immediately without polling a dashboard.

user_actions: Create a Telegram bot via @BotFather — save the token
Get your chat ID: send /start to @userinfobot or add bot to a channel and get channel ID
Add TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID to .env.local and Vercel environment variables
Add same secrets to GitHub repo settings (for GitHub Actions cron)

existing_state.trade_events: stepCandle() emits 'tradeOpened' and 'tradeClosed' events with full Trade object

spec.description: Thin wrapper around Telegram Bot API sendMessage. Fire-and-forget pattern — never throws, never blocks caller.

exports.name: sendTelegramMessage
logic: Read TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID from process.env
If either is missing, return silently (no-op in dev environments)
Body: { chat_id, text, parse_mode: 'HTML', disable_web_page_preview: true }
Wrap entire function in try-catch — on ANY error, console.warn and return (never throw)
Do NOT await this function in the cron route — call it and let it resolve in background

exports.name: formatTradeOpened
logic: Returns HTML-formatted message for trade open notification
notes: Calculate SL distance as percentage from entry
Format prices with appropriate decimals (BTC=2, others=4)

exports.name: formatTradeClosed
logic: Returns HTML-formatted message for trade close notification

exports.name: formatCronSummary
exports.signature: (results: CronResult[], duration: number, tradesClosed: number) => string | null
logic: Returns summary message ONLY when something noteworthy happened
Return null (no message) if: 0 trades closed AND 0 errors
Return message if: trades were closed OR errors occurred
This prevents hourly spam — only sends when there's news
output_template: {errors section if any: ⚠️ Errors: symbol1: message, symbol2: message}

implementation_notes: Use native fetch() — no axios or node-telegram-bot-api dependency needed
AbortController with 5s timeout prevents hanging the cron worker
console.warn on failure, never console.error (it's a notification, not critical path)
Export a TELEGRAM_ENABLED constant: Boolean(process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN && process.env.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID)

steps.title: Hook notifications into cron route

changes.add: import { sendTelegramMessage, formatTradeOpened, formatTradeClosed, formatCronSummary } from '@/lib/telegram';

changes.location: inside the event loop (line 108-129), after tradeClosed event collection
changes.description: Add tradeOpened notification and tradeClosed notification

changes.location: after cron health log insert (after line 202)
changes.description: Send summary notification only when noteworthy (trades closed or errors)
changes.code_pattern:
const summaryMsg = formatCronSummary(results, duration, totalTradesClosed);
if (summaryMsg) sendTelegramMessage(summaryMsg);

critical_rules: DO NOT await sendTelegramMessage() — it must be fire-and-forget to avoid blocking the cron
sendTelegramMessage handles its own errors internally — no try-catch needed in the caller
The cron must NEVER fail because Telegram is down — this is a notification, not a dependency
Trade notifications are sent BEFORE database insert — if DB fails, at least we know the trade happened

steps.title: Add environment variables documentation
steps.content_description: Document all required and optional env vars

local_test.description: Test Telegram sending without running full cron
steps: Create a temporary test script or API route that calls sendTelegramMessage('Test from Helix')
Test with ALLOW_LOCAL_CRON=1: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_SECRET' http://localhost:3000/api/cron/tick

production_test: Deploy to Vercel with Telegram env vars set
If a trade opens/closes, notification should arrive within seconds
If no trade events, no notification should be sent (no spam)

constraints: Zero new npm dependencies — use native fetch() for Telegram API
Fire-and-forget: sendTelegramMessage must NEVER be awaited in the cron route
Fail-safe: Telegram errors are console.warn only, never thrown
5-second timeout on Telegram API calls via AbortController
No notification spam: cron summary only sent when trades closed or errors occurred
parse_mode: HTML (not Markdown) — safer for price strings with special characters
disable_web_page_preview: true — prevent URL preview clutter
Graceful degradation: if TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is not set, all notification functions are no-ops
Do NOT modify the existing cron logic flow — only ADD notification calls alongside existing code
Trade open and close messages are separate (not combined into one message)
